data: Commissioner of Food & Drugs, Food and Drug Administration
Commissioner of Food & Drugs, Food and Drug Administration, Department of Health & Human Services
No reportable value or income.  Subsequent to filing this report on January 28, 2025, I dissolved this entity with the state of Deleware.
Moreover, the official operating agreement of 2020 listed me as Member and a more recent 2024 agreement listed me as Manager.
This company holds only one asset, my personal residence.
Professor, by Courtesy, Johns Hopkins Carey Business School, 2/2021 - Present
Chief, Islet Transplant Surgery, Johns Hopkins Medicine, 6/2013 - Present
Professor, Johns Hopkins University School of Medicine, 7/2003 - Present
Member, Compensation Committee of the Board of Directors, 4/2024 - 1/2025
Chairperson, Nominations & Governance Committee of the Board of Directors, 1/2024 - 1/2025
Member, Nominations & Governance Committee of the Board of Directors, 6/2022 - 1/2025
Member, Audit Committee of the Board of Directors, 6/2022 - 1/2025

Initially, in 2018, I served as a consultant.  From 2018 to 2023, I served as Chief Medical Officer.  In 2023, I transitioned from being Chief Medical Officer to being an advisor.  My duties did not change; only my position title changed.
Advisor (Previously Consultant and Chief Medical Officer), 8/2018 - 12/2024
Fox News Network, LLC (cable network and news channel)
"Blind Spots:  When Medicine Gets It Wrong, and What It Means for Our Health," Bloomsbury Publishing, Inc. (value not readily ascertainable)
"Blind Spots:  When Medicine Gets It Wrong, and What It Means for Our Health," Bloomsbury Publishing, Inc., royalties: advance receivable
"The Price We Pay:  What Broke American Health Care—and How to Fix It," Bloomsbury Publishing, Inc. (value not readily ascertainable)
The date of submission of the article is approximate.  The article was never published.
Johns Hopkins Department of Gynecology & Obstetrics, Johns Hopkins Medicine - 01/11/2024
Global Appropriateness Measures, LLC (health care data analytics)
S2 Bryans Road Associates, LLC (real estate holding company)
This developed area is leased to the University of Maryland, which uses it for a nutrition education center and a primary care clinic for a nurse practitioner.
Received for services provided through Ladner-Drysdale, LLC.
Avalon.AI, LLC (health care scheduling platform), vested equity units
Sidecar Health, Inc. (health insurance), vested stock options
Turquoise Health Co. (health care pricing platform), vested stock options
Advocate, Inc. (benefits brokerage), vested stock options
Sesame, Inc. (health care marketplace), vested stock options
MedRegen, LLC (clinical stage pharmaceutical), vested profits interests
Amaze, PBC (telehealth, health education, and medical billing advocacy)
Vanguard Institutional Index Fund - Institutional Plus Shares (VIIIX)
Vanguard Total Stock Market Index Fund - Institutional Plus Shares (VSMPX)
Schwab Value Advantage Money Fund - Investor Class (SWVXX)
The company will remain dormant during my appointment. (2024-07-01)
I will divest my stock as soon as practicable but not later than 90 days after my confirmation. (2022-06-01)

Pursuant to the terms of my agreement with the company I received equity units as my compensation for services I provided as an advisor.  In December 2024, I resigned from my position with the company.  The company has agreed to buy back my equity units.  I agree that I will complete the divestiture of all of my equity in the company as soon as practicable but not later than 90 days after my confirmation.  The company has agreed to purchase my equity. (2022-04-01)

I will divest my stock options and stock in Sidecar Health, Inc., as soon as practicable but not later than 90 days after my confirmation.  If I divest the stock options by exercising them, I will divest the resulting stock as soon as practicable but not later than 90 days after my confirmation.  The company has agreed to purchase my equity. (2021-07-01)

Pursuant to the terms of my agreement with the company I received stock options as compensation for services I provided to the Advisory Board.  Subsequent to the granting any options, all have vested, and I currently retain only vested stock options  In December 2024, I resigned from my position with the company.  The company has agreed to permit me to sell my private equity in the company.  I agree that I will complete the divestiture of all of my equity in the company within 90 days of my confirmation.  If I divest my stock options by exercising them, I will divest the resulting stock as soon as practicable but not later than 90 days after my confirmation. (2021-07-01)

I will divest my stock options and stock in Advocate, Inc., as soon as practicable but not later than 90 days after my confirmation.  If I divest the stock options by exercising them, I will divest the resulting stock as soon as practicable but not later than 90 days after my confirmation.  The company has agreed to purchase my equity. (2020-09-01)

Pursuant to the terms of my agreement with the company I received stock options as compensation for services I provided to the Advisory Board.  Subsequent to the granting of these stock options, all have vested.  I currently retain only vested stock options.  In December 2024, I resigned from my position with the company.  The company has advised that I am permitted to sell my private equity in the company.  I agree that I will complete the divestiture of all of my equity in the company within 90 days of my confirmation. If I divest my stock options by exercising them, I will divest the resulting stock as soon as practicable but not later than 90 days after my confirmation. (2020-02-01)

Pursuant t to the terms of my agreement with the company I received profit interests as my compensation for services I provided as an advisor.  In December 2024, I resigned from my position with the company.  The company has advised me that I may divest of my private equity profit interests in the company.  I agree that I will complete the divestiture of all of my equity in the company as soon as practicable but not later than 90 days after my confirmation. (2019-06-01)

As part of my compensation for services that I provided to the company I was granted shares of private company stock.  I ceased to provide services to the company in 2020. The company has agreed to buy back my shares of company stock.  I agree that I will divest of my stock in Amaze PBC as soon as practicable but not later than 90 days after my confirmation.  The company has agreed to purchase my equity. (2019-06-01)

I currently retain equity units in Global Appropriateness Measures, LLC (GAM).  I do not hold any other equity interest in GAM.  I will divest my equity units in GAM, as soon as practicable but not later than 90 days after my confirmation. (2016-01-01)

As part of my compensation for services that I provided to the company I was granted private equity options by the company.  During the period of time that I provided services, my options vested and I purchased stock in the company.  I ceased to provide services to the company in 2017. The company has agreed to buy back my private company stock.  I agree that I will divest of my stock in Kyruss, Inc.  as soon as practicable but not later than 90 days after my confirmation.  The company has agreed to purchase my equity. (2014-09-01)
I will continue to retain the 403(b) plan.  The plan sponsor will not make further contributions after my separation. (2004-01-01)
Professor, School of Medicine; Professor, by Courtesy, Johns Hopkins Carey Business School; Chief, Islet Transplant Surgery, Johns Hopkins Medicine
Advisor; Chief Medical Officer (paid through Ladner-Drysdale, LLC; also a client of Global Appropriateness Measures, LLC)
Member, Board of Directors (paid through Ladner-Drysdale, LLC)
Greenwood Health Consulting, LLC (health benefits consulting)
The filer's spouse ceased to provide consulting services to this entity in November 2024.
MemoryWell, Inc. (digital human-to-human health care engagement solution)
Purchased in the open market.  Not related to compensation from Harrow, Inc., which is separately reported in Part 2.
Six Flags Entertainment Corporation (amusement parks operator)
This line reflects income received from Six Flags Entertainment Corporation before a July 2024 merger. Before the merger, it traded under the ticker symbol SIX.
Oddity Tech LTD F Class A (ODD)
Equity interests were vested profit interests.
